readme.text:
# Tekton Pipelines CLI (`tkn`) [![Go Report Card](https://goreportcard.com/badge/tektoncd/cli)](https://goreportcard.com/report/tektoncd/cli) [![CII Best Practices](https://bestpractices.coreinfrastructure.org/projects/6510/badge)](https://bestpractices.coreinfrastructure.org/projects/6510)

The _Tekton Pipelines CLI_ project provides a command-line interface (CLI) for interacting with [Tekton](https://tekton.dev/), an open-source framework for Continuous Integration and Delivery (CI/CD) systems. ## Installing `tkn` Download the latest binary executable for your operating system. ### Mac OS X - Use [Homebrew](https://brew.sh) ```shell brew install tektoncd-cli ``` - Use [released tarball](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Darwin_all.tar.gz) ```shell # Get the tar.xz curl -LO https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Darwin_all.tar.gz # Extract tkn to your PATH (e.g. /usr/local/bin) sudo tar xvzf tkn_0.44.0_Darwin_all.tar.gz -C /usr/local/bin tkn ``` ### Windows - Use [Chocolatey](https://chocolatey.org/packages/tektoncd-cli) ```shell choco install tektoncd-cli --confirm ``` - Use [Scoop](https://scoop.sh) ```powershell scoop install tektoncd-cli ``` - Use [Powershell](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell) [released zip](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Windows_x86_64.zip) ```powershell #Create directory New-Item -Path "$HOME/tektoncd/cli" -Type Directory # Download file Start-BitsTransfer -Source https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Windows_x86_64.zip -Destination "$HOME/tektoncd/cli/." # Uncompress zip file Expand-Archive $HOME/tektoncd/cli/*.zip -DestinationPath C:\Users\Developer\tektoncd\cli\. #Add to Windows `Environment Variables` [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path",$($env:Path + ";$Home\tektoncd\cli"),'User') ``` ### Linux tarballs - [Linux AMD 64](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Linux_x86_64.tar.gz) ```shell # Get the tar.xz curl -LO https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Linux_x86_64.tar.gz # Extract tkn to your PATH (e.g. /usr/local/bin) sudo tar xvzf tkn_0.44.0_Linux_x86_64.tar.gz -C /usr/local/bin/ tkn ``` - [Linux AARCH 64](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Linux_aarch64.tar.gz) ```shell # Get the tar.xz curl -LO https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Linux_aarch64.tar.gz # Extract tkn to your PATH (e.g. /usr/local/bin) sudo tar xvzf tkn_0.44.0_Linux_aarch64.tar.gz -C /usr/local/bin/ tkn ``` - [Linux IBM Z](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Linux_s390x.tar.gz) ```shell # Get the tar.gz curl -LO https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Linux_s390x.tar.gz # Extract tkn to your PATH (e.g. /usr/local/bin) sudo tar xvzf tkn_0.44.0_Linux_s390x.tar.gz -C /usr/local/bin/ tkn ``` - [Linux IBM P](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Linux_ppc64le.tar.gz) ```shell # Get the tar.gz curl -LO https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tkn_0.44.0_Linux_ppc64le.tar.gz # Extract tkn to your PATH (e.g. /usr/local/bin) sudo tar xvzf tkn_0.44.0_Linux_ppc64le.tar.gz -C /usr/local/bin/ tkn ``` ### Linux RPMs If you are running on any of the following rpm based distros: - Latest Fedora and the two versions behind. - Centos Stream - EPEL - Latest RHEL you would be able to use [@chmouel](https://github.com/chmouel)'s unofficial copr package repository by running the following commands: ```shell dnf copr enable chmouel/tektoncd-cli dnf install tektoncd-cli ``` - [Binary RPM package](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tektoncd-cli-0.44.0_Linux-64bit.rpm) On any other RPM based distros, you can install the rpm directly: ```shell rpm -Uvh https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tektoncd-cli-0.44.0_Linux-64bit.rpm ``` ### Linux Debs - [Ubuntu PPA](https://launchpad.net/~tektoncd/+archive/ubuntu/cli/+packages) If you are running on the latest rolling Ubuntu or Debian, you can use the TektonCD CLI PPA: ```shell sudo apt update;sudo apt install -y gnupg sudo apt-key adv --keyserver keyserver.ubuntu.com --recv-keys 3EFE0E0A2F2F60AA echo "deb http://ppa.launchpad.net/tektoncd/cli/ubuntu oracular main"|sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/tektoncd-ubuntu-cli.list sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y tektoncd-cli ``` The PPA may work with older releases, but that hasn't been tested. - [Binary DEB package](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tektoncd-cli-0.44.0_Linux-64bit.deb) On any other Debian or Ubuntu based distro, you can simply install the binary package directly with `dpkg`: ```shell curl -LO https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/releases/download/v0.44.0/tektoncd-cli-0.44.0_Linux-64bit.deb dpkg -i tektoncd-cli-0.44.0_Linux-64bit.deb ``` ### NixOS/Nix You can install `tektoncd-cli` from [nixpkgs](https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs) on any system that supports the `nix` package manager. ```shell nix-env --install tektoncd-cli ``` ### Arch / Manjaro You can install [`tekton-cli`](https://archlinux.org/packages/extra/x86_64/tekton-cli/) from the official arch package repository : ```shell pacman -S tekton-cli ``` ### Homebrew on Linux You can install the latest tektoncd-cli if you are using [Homebrew on Linux](https://docs.brew.sh/Homebrew-on-Linux) as for the osx version you need to simply do : ```shell brew install tektoncd-cli ``` ### Source install If you have [go](https://golang.org/) installed and you want to compile the CLI from source, you can checkout the [Git repository](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli) and run the following commands: ```shell make bin/tkn ``` This will output the `tkn` binary in `bin/tkn` ### `tkn` as a `kubectl` plugin `kubectl` will find any binary named `kubectl-*` on your PATH and consider it as a plugin. After installing tkn, create a link as kubectl-tkn ```shell ln -s /usr/local/bin/tkn /usr/local/bin/kubectl-tkn ``` For Mac OS X with Homebrew ```shell ln -s $(brew --prefix)/opt/tektoncd-cli/bin/tkn /usr/local/bin/kubectl-tkn ``` Run the following to confirm tkn is available as a plugin: ```shell kubectl plugin list ``` You should see the following after running kubectl plugin list if tkn is available as a plugin: ```shell /usr/local/bin/kubectl-tkn ``` If the output above is shown, run kubectl-tkn to see the list of available tkn commands to run. ## Useful Commands The following commands help you understand and effectively use the Tekton CLI: - `tkn help:` Displays a list of the commands with helpful information. - [`tkn bundle:`](docs/cmd/tkn_bundle.md) Manage Tekton [bundles](https://github.com/tektoncd/pipeline/blob/main/docs/tekton-bundle-contracts.md) - [`tkn clustertriggerbinding:`](docs/cmd/tkn_clustertriggerbinding.md) Parent command of the ClusterTriggerBinding command group. - [`tkn completion:`](docs/cmd/tkn_completion.md) Outputs a BASH, ZSH, Fish or PowerShell completion script for `tkn` to allow command completion with Tab. - [`tkn customrun:`](docs/cmd/tkn_customrun.md) Parent command of the Customrun command group. - [`tkn eventlistener:`](docs/cmd/tkn_eventlistener.md) Parent command of the Eventlistener command group. - [`tkn hub:`](docs/cmd/tkn_hub.md) Search and install Tekton Resources from [Hub](https://hub.tekton.dev) - [`tkn pipeline:`](docs/cmd/tkn_pipeline.md) Parent command of the Pipeline command group. - [`tkn pipelinerun:`](docs/cmd/tkn_pipelinerun.md) Parent command of the Pipelinerun command group. - [`tkn task:`](docs/cmd/tkn_task.md) Parent command of the Task command group. - [`tkn taskrun:`](docs/cmd/tkn_taskrun.md) Parent command of the Taskrun command group. - [`tkn triggerbinding:`](docs/cmd/tkn_triggerbinding.md) Parent command of the Triggerbinding command group. - [`tkn triggertemplate:`](docs/cmd/tkn_triggertemplate.md) Parent command of the Triggertemplate command group. - [`tkn version:`](docs/cmd/tkn_version.md) Outputs the cli version. For every `tkn` command, you can use `-h` or `--help` flags to display specific help for that command. ## Disable Color and Emojis in Output For many `tkn` commands, color and emojis by default will appear in command output. It will only shows if you are in interactive shell with a [standard input]() attached. If you pipe the tkn command or run it in a non interactive way (ie: from tekton itself in a Task) the coloring and emojis will **always** be disabled. `tkn` offers two approaches for disabling color and emojis from command output. To remove the color and emojis from all `tkn` command output, set the environment variable `NO_COLOR`, such as shown below: ```shell export NO_COLOR="" ``` More information on `NO_COLOR` can be found in the [`NO_COLOR` documentation](https://no-color.org/). To remove color and emojis from the output of a single command execution, the `--no-color` option can be used with any command, such as in the example below: ```bash tkn taskrun describe --no-color ``` ## Want to contribute We are so excited to have you! - See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for an overview of our processes - See [DEVELOPMENT.md](DEVELOPMENT.md) for how to get started - See [ROADMAP.md](ROADMAP.md) for the current roadmap - See [releases.md][releases.md] for our release cadence and processes - Look at our [good first issues](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3A%22good+first+issue%22) and our [help wanted issues](https://github.com/tektoncd/cli/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+label%3A%22help+wanted%22)

readme.text:
# Mailbox

[![Tests](https://github.com/harryzcy/mailbox/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/harryzcy/mailbox/actions)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/harryzcy/mailbox/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/harryzcy/mailbox)
[![Go Report Card](https://goreportcard.com/badge/github.com/harryzcy/mailbox)](https://goreportcard.com/report/github.com/harryzcy/mailbox)
[![PRs Welcome](https://img.shields.io/badge/PRs-welcome-brightgreen.svg?style=flat)](http://makeapullrequest.com)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/github/license/harryzcy/mailbox)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)

Docs: [English](README.md) • [简体中文](README_zh.md)

Versatile email infrastructure that operates on AWS.

## Clients

### Web

See [mailbox-browser](https://github.com/harryzcy/mailbox-browser)

| Dark mode |  Light mode |
|:---------:|:-----------:|
| ![Screenshot Dark Mode](https://github.com/harryzcy/mailbox-browser/assets/37034805/b77a6c40-c6c1-4dd8-98de-2add697b26f9) | ![Screenshot Light Mode](https://github.com/harryzcy/mailbox-browser/assets/37034805/ce9ab42c-923a-4b03-8ee4-bcdc9d4b72ed) |

### CLI

```bash
go install github.com/harryzcy/mailbox-cli
```

For details, refer to [mailbox-cli](https://github.com/harryzcy/mailbox-cli)

## Deploy

1. Clone the repository.

    ```shell
    git clone https://github.com/harryzcy/mailbox
    ```

1. Install [serverless](https://github.com/serverless/serverless).

    ```shell
    npm install -g serverless@v3
    ```

1. Create an IAM user.

    Create an IAM user with AdministratorAccess and export the access key as environment variables.

    ```shell
    export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=<your-key-here>
    export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=<your-secret-key-here>
    ```

    In production, setup the IAM user following [this guide from serverless](https://www.serverless.com/framework/docs/providers/aws/guide/credentials).

1. Setup AWS services.

    Manually create S3 buckets, and setup SES and SQS (optional) from AWS console.

1. Copy over example configurations and fill in correct fields.

    ```shell
    cp serverless.yml.example serverless.yml
    ```

    Under `provider.environment` section, modify `REGION`, `S3_BUCKET`, `SQS_QUEUE` (optional, only if SQS should be enabled).

1. Deploy the app.

    ```shell
    make deploy
    ```

1. Configure email receiving.

    From AWS console -> Configuration -> Email receiving -> Create rule set -> Create rule, add two actions:

    1. Deliver to Amazon S3 bucket, then enter your bucket name.
    2. Invoke AWS Lambda function, and select `mailbox-dev-emailReceive` or `mailbox-prod-emailReceive`.

1. Deploy [mailbox-browser](https://github.com/harryzcy/mailbox-browser) or use [mailbox-cli](https://github.com/harryzcy/mailbox-cli).

## API

See [doc/API.md](doc/api.md)

## Architecture

It runs on AWS services, including SES, Lambda, API Gateway, DynamoDB, and SQS.

![Architecture](./doc/architecture.svg)

## Contributing

### Development environment

- Go >= 1.25

Note that only the two most recent minor versions of Go are officially supported.

readme.text:
# boss-agent-cli **专为 AI Agent 设计的 BOSS 直聘求职 CLI 工具** > 搜索职位 · 福利筛选 · 个性化推荐 · 自动打招呼 · 求职流水线 · 增量监控 · AI 简历优化 [![CI](https://github.com/can4hou6joeng4/boss-agent-cli/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/can4hou6joeng4/boss-agent-cli/actions/workflows/ci.yml) [![Coverage](https://codecov.io/gh/can4hou6joeng4/boss-agent-cli/branch/master/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/can4hou6joeng4/boss-agent-cli) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-≥3.10-3776AB?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://python.org) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green.svg)](LICENSE) [![GitHub Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/can4hou6joeng4/boss-agent-cli)](https://github.com/can4hou6joeng4/boss-agent-cli/releases) [![PRs Welcome](https://img.shields.io/badge/PRs-welcome-brightgreen.svg)](https://github.com/can4hou6joeng4/boss-agent-cli/pulls) [![Open in Codespaces](https://github.com/codespaces/badge.svg)](https://codespaces.new/can4hou6joeng4/boss-agent-cli) [安装](#-安装) · [快速开始](#-快速开始) · [Agent 集成](#-ai-agent-集成) · [命令参考](#-命令参考) · [架构](#-技术架构) · [更新日志](CHANGELOG.md) · [路线图](ROADMAP.md) **中文** | [English](README.en.md)
> A CLI tool designed for AI Agents to interact with [BOSS Zhipin](https://www.zhipin.com/) (China's largest recruitment platform). All output is structured JSON, 34 commands, 4-tier login fallback. See [README.en.md](README.en.md) for the English version. --- ## 💡 为什么用 boss-agent-cli？ 传统求职：打开网页 → 翻几十页 → 逐个看详情 → 手动打招呼 → 忘了跟进谁。 **boss-agent-cli 让 AI Agent 替你完成全部操作**： ```bash boss search "Golang" --city 广州 --welfare "双休,五险一金" # 搜索 + 福利筛选 boss detail # 查看详情 boss greet # 一键打招呼 boss pipeline # 流水线追踪 boss digest # 每日汇报 ``` 所有输出为 **结构化 JSON**，Agent 一调用就能理解，一调用就能行动。 --- ## 🌟 核心能力 | 能力 | 说明 | 命令 | |------|------|------| | 🔍 **职位发现** | 关键词搜索 + 8 维筛选 + 个性化推荐 | `search` `recommend` | | 🎯 **福利筛选** | `--welfare "双休,五险一金"` 自动翻页逐条匹配 | `search --welfare` | | 👋 **主动出击** | 单个/批量打招呼、立即沟通投递 | `greet` `batch-greet` `apply` | | 📊 **进度管理** | 求职流水线 + 跟进提醒 + 每日摘要 | `pipeline` `follow-up` `digest` | | 👀 **增量监控** | 保存搜索条件、定期执行、标出新职位 | `watch` `shortlist` `preset` | | 💬 **沟通管理** | 聊天记录、结构化摘要、标签管理 | `chat` `chatmsg` `chat-summary` | | 🤖 **AI 优化** | 岗位分析 + 简历润色 + 匹配优化 + 面试准备 + 沟通指导 | `ai analyze-jd` `ai polish` `ai optimize` `ai interview-prep` `ai chat-coach` | | 📤 **数据导出** | CSV / JSON / HTML / Markdown 多格式 | `export` `chat --export` | --- ## 📦 安装 ```bash # 推荐：通过 uv 安装（秒级，自动隔离） uv tool install boss-agent-cli # 安装浏览器（用于登录） patchright install chromium ```
📋 其他安装方式 ```bash # pipx（隔离环境） pipx install boss-agent-cli patchright install chromium # pip pip install boss-agent-cli patchright install chromium # 从源码（开发用） git clone https://github.com/can4hou6joeng4/boss-agent-cli.git cd boss-agent-cli uv sync --all-extras uv run patchright install chromium ```
--- ## 🚀 快速开始 ```bash # 1. 环境自检 boss doctor # 2. 登录（自动四级降级） boss login # 3. 验证登录态 boss status # 4. 搜索广州的 Golang 职位，要求双休+五险一金 boss search "Golang" --city 广州 --welfare "双休,五险一金" # 5. 查看详情 → 打招呼 → 投递 boss detail boss greet boss apply # 6. 推荐 + 导出 boss recommend boss export "Golang" --city 广州 --count 50 -o jobs.csv # 7. 流水线 + 每日摘要 boss pipeline boss digest # 8. 增量监控 boss watch add my-golang "Golang" --city 广州 --welfare "双休" boss watch run my-golang ``` --- ## 🔐 登录链路 `boss login` 采用**四级降级策略**，适配不同环境： | 级别 | 方式 | 说明 | 需要浏览器？ | |:---:|------|------|:---:| | 1 | **Cookie 提取** | 从本地 Chrome/Firefox/Edge 等 10+ 浏览器免扫码提取 | 否 | | 2 | **CDP 登录** | 复用带 `--remote-debugging-port` 的 Chrome | 需 Chrome | | 3 | **QR httpx** | 纯 HTTP 二维码扫码，无需安装任何浏览器 | 否 | | 4 | **patchright** | 反检测 Chromium 兜底 | 需 Chromium |
📖 CDP 启动示例 ```bash # macOS /Applications/Google\ Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google\ Chrome \ --remote-debugging-port=9222 --user-data-dir=/tmp/boss-chrome # Linux google-chrome --remote-debugging-port=9222 --user-data-dir=/tmp/boss-chrome # 使用 CDP 登录 boss --cdp-url http://localhost:9222 login --cdp ```
--- ## 🤖 AI Agent 集成 推荐先阅读：[Agent Quickstart](docs/agent-quickstart.md) · [Host Examples](docs/agent-hosts.md) · [Capability Matrix](docs/capability-matrix.md) ### 方式一：Skill 安装（推荐） ```bash npx skills add can4hou6joeng4/boss-agent-cli ``` 安装后 Agent 自动获得调用 `boss` 命令的能力，无需手动配置。 ### 方式二：手动配置 在 AI Agent 的规则文件中添加： ```markdown 当用户要求搜索职位、投递、打招呼等 BOSS 直聘操作时，通过 Bash 调用 `boss` CLI： 1. 运行 `boss status` 检查登录态 2. 若未登录，运行 `boss login` 提示用户扫码 3. 根据用户意图调用 search / recommend / detail / greet 4. 解析 stdout JSON，`ok` 字段判断成败 5. 用户提到福利要求时使用 `--welfare` 参数 ``` ### 方式三：Python 直接嵌入（不走 subprocess） 包已随 `py.typed` 标记发布，可直接作为类型化的 Python 库使用： ```python from boss_agent_cli import AuthManager, BossClient, AuthRequired auth = AuthManager(data_dir=Path("~/.boss-agent").expanduser()) try: with BossClient(auth) as client: result = client.search_jobs("Golang", city="广州") except AuthRequired: ... # 提示用户 boss login ``` 公开 API（详见 `boss_agent_cli.__all__`）：`AuthManager` / `BossClient` / `CacheStore` / `JobItem` / `JobDetail` / `AIService` / `ResumeData` 等核心类型。 ### 输出协议 所有命令输出 JSON 到 stdout，统一信封格式： ```json { "ok": true, "schema_version": "1.0", "command": "search", "data": [...], "pagination": {"page": 1, "has_more": true, "total": 15}, "error": null, "hints": {"next_actions": ["boss detail "]} } ``` | 约定 | 说明 | |------|------| | `stdout` | 仅 JSON 结构化数据 | | `stderr` | 日志和进度信息 | | `exit 0` | 命令成功 (`ok=true`) | | `exit 1` | 命令失败 (`ok=false`) | --- ## 📚 命令参考 ### 基础操作 | 命令 | 说明 | |------|------| | `boss schema` | 输出 32 个命令的完整能力描述（Agent 首先调用） | | `boss login` | 四级降级登录 | | `boss logout` | 退出登录 | | `boss status` | 检查登录态 | | `boss doctor` | 诊断环境、依赖、凭据完整性和网络 | | `boss me` | 我的信息（用户/简历/期望/投递记录） | ### 职位搜索 | 命令 | 说明 | |------|------| | `boss search ` | 搜索职位（支持 `--welfare` 筛选、`--preset` 预设） | | `boss recommend` | 个性化推荐 | | `boss detail ` | 职位详情（`--job-id` 走快速通道） | | `boss show <#>` | 按编号查看上次搜索结果 | | `boss cities` | 40 个支持城市 | ### 求职动作 | 命令 | 说明 | |------|------| | `boss greet ` | 打招呼 | | `boss batch-greet ` | 批量打招呼（上限 10） | | `boss apply ` | 投递/立即沟通（幂等） | | `boss exchange ` | 交换手机/微信 | ### 沟通跟进 | 命令 | 说明 | |------|------| | `boss chat` | 沟通列表（导出 html/md/csv/json） | | `boss chatmsg ` | 聊天消息历史 | | `boss chat-summary ` | 结构化摘要 | | `boss mark --label X` | 标签管理（9 种） | | `boss interviews` | 面试邀请 | | `boss history` | 浏览历史 | ### 流水线监控 | 命令 | 说明 | |------|------| | `boss pipeline` | 求职流水线（各阶段状态） | | `boss follow-up` | 跟进提醒（超时未推进） | | `boss digest` | 每日摘要 | | `boss watch add/list/remove/run` | 增量监控 | | `boss shortlist add/list/remove` | 候选池 | | `boss preset add/list/remove` | 搜索预设 | ### 简历与 AI | 命令 | 说明 | |------|------| | `boss resume init/list/show/edit/delete/export/import/clone/diff` | 本地简历管理 | | `boss ai config` | 配置 AI 服务 | | `boss ai analyze-jd` | 分析岗位要求 | | `boss ai polish` | 润色简历 | | `boss ai optimize` | 针对目标岗位优化 | | `boss ai suggest` | 求职建议 | | `boss ai reply` | 生成招聘者消息回复草稿 | | `boss ai interview-prep` | 基于 JD 生成模拟面试题 | | `boss ai chat-coach` | 基于聊天记录给沟通建议 | > 支持 Claude 4.7 / GPT-5 / DeepSeek-V3 / Qwen3 等最新模型，详见 [推荐模型与入口](docs/integrations/ai-models.md)。 ### 系统管理 | 命令 | 说明 | |------|------| | `boss config list/set/reset` | 配置管理 | | `boss clean` | 清理缓存 | | `boss export ` | 导出结果（CSV/JSON） |
🔎 搜索筛选参数详解 ```bash boss search "golang" \ --city 广州 \ # 城市（40 个可选） --salary 20-50K \ # 薪资范围 --experience 3-5年 \ # 经验要求 --education 本科 \ # 学历要求 --scale 100-499人 \ # 公司规模 --industry 互联网 \ # 行业 --stage 已上市 \ # 融资阶段 --welfare "双休,五险一金" # 福利筛选（AND 逻辑） ``` **福利筛选工作原理**： 1. 先检查职位福利标签（`welfareList`） 2. 标签不匹配时自动获取职位描述全文搜索 3. 自动翻页（最多 5 页） 4. 每个结果带 `welfare_match` 说明匹配来源 支持关键词：`双休` `五险一金` `年终奖` `餐补` `住房补贴` `定期体检` `股票期权` `加班补助` `带薪年假`
--- ## 🔧 诊断与排障 ```bash boss doctor ``` | 检查项 | 说明 | |--------|------| | `python` | Python 版本 >= 3.10 | | `patchright` | CLI 已安装 | | `patchright_chromium` | Chromium 已安装 | | `cookie_extract` | 本地浏览器 Cookie 可提取 | | `auth_session` | 登录态存在且可解密 | | `auth_token_quality` | 核心凭据（wt2 / stoken） | | `cookie_completeness` | 辅助凭据（wbg / zp_at） | | `cdp` | Chrome 调试端口可连 | | `network` | zhipin.com 可访问 |
📖 常见问题修复 ```bash # 安装浏览器内核 patchright install chromium # 重建登录态 boss logout && boss login # CDP 诊断 boss --cdp-url http://localhost:9222 doctor ``` **auth_session 显示"损坏"**：登录态来自旧机器指纹或文件损坏 → `boss logout && boss login` **auth_token_quality 各状态含义**： - `wt2/stoken 均存在`：完整，可正常使用 - `wt2 存在，stoken 缺失`：部分可用，接口失败时 `boss login` 刷新 - `wt2 缺失`：无效 → `boss logout && boss login`
📖 错误码与自动修复 | 错误码 | 含义 | Agent 自动修复 | |--------|------|---------------| | `AUTH_REQUIRED` | 未登录 | `boss login` | | `AUTH_EXPIRED` | 登录过期 | `boss login` | | `RATE_LIMITED` | 频率过高 | 等待后重试 | | `TOKEN_REFRESH_FAILED` | Token 刷新失败 | `boss login` | | `ACCOUNT_RISK` | 风控拦截 | CDP Chrome 重试 | | `INVALID_PARAM` | 参数错误 | 修正参数 | | `ALREADY_GREETED` | 已打过招呼 | 跳过 | | `GREET_LIMIT` | 今日次数用完 | 告知用户 | | `NETWORK_ERROR` | 网络错误 | 重试 | | `AI_NOT_CONFIGURED` | AI 未配置 | `boss ai config` |
--- ## ⚙️ 配置 ```bash boss config list # 查看所有配置 boss config set default_city 广州 # 设置默认城市 boss config reset # 恢复默认 ```
📖 完整配置项 `~/.boss-agent/config.json`： ```json { "default_city": null, "default_salary": null, "request_delay": [1.5, 3.0], "batch_greet_delay": [2.0, 5.0], "batch_greet_max": 10, "log_level": "error", "login_timeout": 120, "cdp_url": null, "export_dir": null } ``` | 配置项 | 说明 | |--------|------| | `default_city` | 默认城市 | | `default_salary` | 默认薪资范围 | | `request_delay` | 请求间隔（秒），`[min, max]` | | `batch_greet_delay` | 批量打招呼间隔 | | `batch_greet_max` | 批量打招呼上限 | | `log_level` | 日志级别（error/warning/info/debug） | | `login_timeout` | 登录超时（秒） | | `cdp_url` | CDP 地址 | | `export_dir` | 导出目录 |
--- ## 🏗️ 技术架构 ``` CLI (Click) │ ├── AuthManager ── Cookie 提取 / CDP / QR httpx / patchright │ └── TokenStore (Fernet + PBKDF2 机器绑定加密) │ ├── BossClient ── httpx (低风险) + 浏览器 (高风险) 混合通道 │ ├── RequestThrottle (高斯延迟 + 突发惩罚) │ ├── BrowserSession (CDP / Bridge / patchright) │ └── BOSS 直聘 wapi (18+ 端点) │ ├── CacheStore (SQLite WAL) ├── AIService (OpenAI / Anthropic / 兼容 API) └── output.py → JSON 信封 → stdout ``` | 层级 | 选型 | |------|------| | 语言 | Python >= 3.10 | | CLI | Click | | HTTP | httpx | | 浏览器 | patchright（Playwright 反检测 fork） | | Cookie | browser-cookie3（10+ 浏览器） | | 加密 | cryptography (Fernet + PBKDF2) | | 数据库 | sqlite3 (WAL 模式) | | 渲染 | rich | | AI | OpenAI / Anthropic Chat Completions API | | 测试 | pytest（666 项） | --- ## 🤝 贡献 欢迎提交 Issue 和 Pull Request。 ```bash # 本地开发 git clone https://github.com/can4hou6joeng4/boss-agent-cli.git cd boss-agent-cli uv sync --all-extras uv run pytest tests/ -v # 运行测试 uv run ruff check src/ # 代码检查 ``` 详见 [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) --- ## 🙏 致谢 - [geekgeekrun](https://github.com/geekgeekrun/geekgeekrun) — 浏览器自动化 + 反检测策略 - [boss-cli](https://github.com/jackwener/boss-cli) — CLI 结构化输出 + Agent 友好设计 - [opencli](https://github.com/jackwener/opencli) — Browser Bridge 架构理念 --- ## ⚠️ 免责声明 本项目仅用于学习交流，使用时请遵守相关法律法规及 BOSS 直聘平台用户协议。因不当使用产生的一切后果由使用者自行承担，与本项目作者无关。 --- ## 📑 许可证 [MIT](LICENSE) ## 👭 友情链接 - [LINUX DO - 新的理想型社区](https://linux.do/)

repo_cards.description: The video search layer for AI agents. Search video by meaning — across speech, visuals, and on-screen text.

readme.text:
Cerul

The video search layer for AI agents.

Teach your AI agents to see. Search video by meaning — across speech, visuals, and on-screen text.

Docs · API Reference · Pricing · · ·




## Why Cerul Web pages are easy for AI agents to search. **Video is not.** Most video search today is limited to transcripts — what was *said*. Cerul goes further by indexing what is *shown*: slides, charts, product demos, code walkthroughs, whiteboards, and other visual evidence. > [!NOTE] > Cerul is in active development. The API is live at [cerul.ai](https://cerul.ai) — sign up to get a free API key. ## Quickstart ### Claude Code plugin Install the official Cerul plugin in Claude Code: ```bash /plugin marketplace add cerul-ai/cerul-plugin-cc /plugin install cerul@cerul-plugin ``` Source: [cerul-ai/cerul-plugin-cc](https://github.com/cerul-ai/cerul-plugin-cc). ### Use with your AI agent Copy this and send it to your agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.): ``` Install the Cerul video search skill: mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/cerul && curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cerul-ai/cerul/main/skills/cerul/SKILL.md -o ~/.claude/skills/cerul/SKILL.md && cat ~/.claude/skills/cerul/SKILL.md ``` Your agent will install the CLI, set up credentials, and start searching videos as a tool.
Or install via the skills CLI: ```bash npx skills add cerul-ai/cerul ```
Install for specific agents ```bash # Claude Code mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/cerul && curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cerul-ai/cerul/main/skills/cerul/SKILL.md -o ~/.claude/skills/cerul/SKILL.md # Windsurf mkdir -p ~/.codeium/windsurf/skills/cerul && curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cerul-ai/cerul/main/skills/cerul/SKILL.md -o ~/.codeium/windsurf/skills/cerul/SKILL.md # OpenCode mkdir -p ~/.config/opencode/skills/cerul && curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cerul-ai/cerul/main/skills/cerul/SKILL.md -o ~/.config/opencode/skills/cerul/SKILL.md # Cline / Cursor mkdir -p .claude/skills/cerul && curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cerul-ai/cerul/main/skills/cerul/SKILL.md -o .claude/skills/cerul/SKILL.md ```
### CLI ```bash curl -fsSL https://cli.cerul.ai/install.sh | bash cerul search "Sam Altman on AI video generation tools" ```
> Inline video frame previews are supported in iTerm2, WezTerm, and Kitty. Enable with `cerul config` and toggle **Images** on. Other terminals show text-only results. ### MCP (Model Context Protocol) Connect any MCP-compatible client to the hosted endpoint (replace with your API key): ```bash # Claude Code claude mcp add --transport streamable-http "https://api.cerul.ai/mcp?apiKey=YOUR_API_KEY" cerul # Codex codex mcp add --url "https://api.cerul.ai/mcp?apiKey=YOUR_API_KEY" cerul ``` Also works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, and other MCP clients.
SDK & API **Python** ```bash pip install cerul ``` ```python from cerul import Cerul client = Cerul(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY") results = client.search(query="Sam Altman on AGI timeline", max_results=5) for r in results: print(r.title, r.url) ``` **JavaScript** ```bash npm install cerul ``` ```javascript import { cerul } from "cerul"; const client = cerul({ apiKey: "YOUR_API_KEY" }); const result = await client.search({ query: "Sam Altman on AGI timeline", max_results: 5 }); for (const r of result.results) { console.log(r.title, r.url); } ``` **cURL** ```bash curl "https://api.cerul.ai/v1/search" \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY" \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{"query": "Sam Altman on AGI timeline", "max_results": 5}' ``` Full API spec: [`openapi.yaml`](./openapi.yaml) | [cerul.ai/docs](https://cerul.ai/docs/search-api)
## License Licensed under [Apache 2.0](./LICENSE).

Community

Join our WeChat group to chat with the team and other users. Scan the QR code below (refreshes weekly):
Or join us on [Discord](https://discord.gg/qHDEMQB9vN) for English discussion.

[![Star History Chart](https://api.star-history.com/svg?repos=cerul-ai/cerul&type=Date)](https://star-history.com/#cerul-ai/cerul&Date)
Built by @JessyTsui

items.name: v0.0.03 — API Playground, Tracking Refactor & Admin Analytics
items.body_excerpt:
## What's New

### API Playground
- Tavily-inspired search playground at `/dashboard/playground`
- Code generation with syntax highlighting (Python / JavaScript / Shell / Go)
- Per-result preview with clickable video links and thumbs up/down feedback
- Collapsible advanced parameters: ranking mode, filters, max results

### Tracking Links Refactor
- Permanent `short_id` on `retrieval_units` — search requests no longer write redirect rows
- Redirect URLs built dynamically from current video data (never stale)
- Legacy tracking_links fallback for old links
- `query_logs.results_preview` for request-level result snapshots

### Admin Analytics
- New `/admin/analytics` page with impression-aware CTR metrics
- Content and creator performance leaderboards
- Search quality views: top queries, empty results, position CTR
- Playground feedback analysis

### API Key UX
- Key prefix changed to `cerul_` (backwards compatible with `cerul_sk_`)
- Raw keys stored for later viewing (eye icon reveal, copy full key)
- Default key auto-created on signup
- Last active key cannot be deleted

### Dashboard Improvements
- Top nav buttons fixed (no more dark overlay bleed-through)
- Create key dialog rounded corners and proper z-index
- Viewport-fixed playground layout with internal scrolling

### Migrations
- `015_playground_feedback` — feedback table
- `016_tracking_query_snapshots` — permanent short_ids + result snapshots
- `017_query_log_search_surface` — API vs playground attribution
- `018_api_key_raw_storage` — raw key persistence

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/cerul-ai/cerul/compare/v0.0.02...v0.0.03

scout.summary: The tektoncd/cli repository is an active Go-based CLI tool for Tekton CI/CD, with recent patch releases addressing multiple CVEs. It provides multi-platform installation instructions and follows standard open-source practices.
highlights: Recent releases (v0.44.1, v0.37.5, v0.37.4) patch multiple CVEs, indicating active security maintenance.
Clear multi-platform installation guides for Linux, macOS, and Windows are provided in the README.
Project adheres to open-source best practices, evidenced by CII Best Practices badge and structured documentation.
risks: Presence of multiple CVEs across recent versions (e.g., CVE-2026-34986, CVE-2026-33186, CVE-2025-61726) suggests potential recurring security issues.
Reliance on external dependencies (evident from go.mod and vendor directory) may introduce supply chain risks if not rigorously audited.

inventor.summary: The tektoncd/cli repository invents and maintains a cross-platform command-line interface for the Tekton CI/CD framework, enabling streamlined interaction with pipelines across Linux, macOS, and Windows environments.
highlights: Invents a unified CLI (tkn) for managing Tekton resources, abstracting platform-specific operations into consistent commands.
Provides native installation bundles for all major operating systems (Linux, macOS, Windows), enhancing accessibility and adoption.
Actively patches security vulnerabilities via frequent patch releases, demonstrating responsive maintenance of the invented tooling.
risks: Repeated CVE disclosures (e.g., CVE-2026-34986 across v0.37.5 and v0.44.1) suggest potential design or dependency flaws in the invented CLI architecture.
Platform-specific installation scripts (e.g., PowerShell environment manipulation) may introduce inconsistent user experiences or system configuration risks.

engineer.summary: The tektoncd/cli repository implements a Go-based CLI (tkn) for interacting with Tekton CI/CD pipelines, supporting Linux, macOS, and Windows. It follows standard engineering practices including automated releases, dependency management, and active CVE patching.
highlights: Multi-platform installation is well-supported with clear instructions and pre-built binaries for Linux, macOS, and Windows (E-RD-004, E-RD-005, E-RD-006, E-RD-007, E-RD-008).
Recent patch releases (v0.37.4, v0.37.5, v0.44.1) actively address multiple CVEs, indicating responsive security maintenance (E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003).
Project structure includes standard Go tooling (go.mod, Makefile), CI configuration (.golangci.yml, .pre-commit-config.yaml), and release automation (.goreleaser.yml) (E-RF-001).
risks: Repeated appearance of the same CVEs (e.g., CVE-2026-34986 and CVE-2026-33186 in both v0.37.5 and v0.44.1) suggests incomplete fixes or shared vulnerable dependencies across versions (E-RL-001, E-RL-002).
Use of vendored dependencies (vendor/ directory) increases binary size and may delay propagation of upstream security patches unless regularly audited (E-RF-001).

skeptic.summary: While the tektoncd/cli repository demonstrates active maintenance and multi-platform support, recurring CVEs across versions and inconsistent patching raise concerns about underlying security hygiene and release integrity.
highlights: The same CVEs (CVE-2026-34986 and CVE-2026-33186) appear in both v0.37.5 and v0.44.1, suggesting either incomplete remediation or poor version isolation.
Installation instructions for Windows involve manual environment variable manipulation via PowerShell, which is error-prone and lacks verification steps.
Despite badges like CII Best Practices, the repeated emergence of high-severity CVEs undermines claims of robust security practices.
risks: Recurring CVEs indicate systemic vulnerabilities, possibly due to shared dependencies or insufficient regression testing during patching.
Lack of cryptographic verification (e.g., checksums or signatures) for downloaded binaries exposes users to supply chain tampering.
Patch releases reference only commit hashes without detailed vulnerability disclosures, limiting user trust and auditability.

scout.summary: The repository implements a serverless email infrastructure on AWS using Go, with active maintenance, CI/CD integration, and clear deployment instructions. It leverages AWS services like SES, S3, Lambda, and optionally SQS/DynamoDB.
highlights: Actively maintained with recent releases featuring dependency updates, ARM64 support, and linting fixes.
Comprehensive CI/CD setup with test workflows, Codecov, Go Report Card, and automated dependency management via Renovate.
Clear deployment guidance for AWS, including IAM setup, environment configuration, and SES rule creation.
Serverless architecture using AWS Lambda, API Gateway, S3, SES, and DynamoDB as indicated by topics and README.
risks: README instructs users to create an IAM user with AdministratorAccess, which violates least-privilege security best practices.
Production deployment relies on external Serverless Framework guide for credentials, potentially leading to misconfiguration if not followed carefully.

inventor.summary: The repository implements a novel serverless email receiving and storage system on AWS using Go, combining SES for email ingestion, S3 for durable storage, Lambda for processing, and optional SQS/DynamoDB for queuing and metadata. It introduces a streamlined, self-hosted mailbox service with dark/light UI modes and infrastructure-as-code via Terraform and Serverless Framework.
highlights: Invents a cohesive serverless email pipeline using AWS SES → S3 → Lambda, with optional SQS integration for asynchronous handling.
Provides a user-facing mailbox interface with theme support (dark/light mode), suggesting an integrated frontend component.
Employs infrastructure-as-code through Terraform (.tf files) and Serverless Framework (serverless.yml.example), enabling reproducible deployments.
Demonstrates architectural evolution through refactoring (e.g., renaming 'types' to 'model' package) and platform expansion (ARM64 builds).
risks: Overprivileged IAM setup instructions (AdministratorAccess) could lead to security vulnerabilities if adopted in production without refinement.
Tight coupling to AWS services may limit portability to other cloud providers or on-premises environments.

engineer.summary: The repository implements a Go-based serverless email ingestion and storage system on AWS, using SES, S3, Lambda, and optional SQS/DynamoDB. It features active maintenance, CI/CD automation, infrastructure-as-code (Terraform/Serverless), and clear deployment instructions—but includes a critical security risk due to overprivileged IAM guidance.
highlights: Robust CI/CD pipeline with test workflows, Codecov coverage, Go Report Card, and automated dependency updates via Renovate bot.
Infrastructure-as-code support through Terraform (.tf files) and Serverless Framework (serverless.yml.example) enables reproducible deployments.
Recent engineering efforts include ARM64 build support, package refactoring (types → model), linting fixes, and Go version upgrades.
Deployment process clearly documented: SES rule setup, S3 delivery, Lambda invocation, and environment configuration for AWS services.
risks: README instructs users to create an IAM user with AdministratorAccess (E-RD-003), violating least-privilege principles and posing significant security risk in production.
Production credential setup defers to external Serverless Framework guide (E-RD-005), which may lead to misconfiguration if not carefully followed.

skeptic.summary: While the repository presents a functional serverless email system on AWS with active maintenance and clear documentation, it promotes insecure IAM practices and lacks evidence of real-world adoption or security hardening beyond basic linting and dependency updates.
highlights: Documentation explicitly instructs users to create an IAM user with AdministratorAccess, which is a severe anti-pattern for production systems.
Despite frequent dependency updates and CI/CD badges, there is no evidence of security audits, threat modeling, or least-privilege IAM policy examples.
The project has only 251 stars and 15 forks, suggesting limited community validation despite being positioned as a self-hosted mailbox solution.
Release notes focus heavily on maintenance and tooling (e.g., runner OS bumps, lint fixes) rather than feature depth, scalability testing, or resilience improvements.
risks: Use of AdministratorAccess in setup instructions (E-RD-003) could lead to catastrophic credential compromise if deployed as-is in production.
No indication of input validation, rate limiting, or abuse prevention for email ingestion—critical for any public-facing email receiver.
Heavy reliance on Serverless Framework and Terraform without clear guidance on securing state files or managing secrets securely.
Lack of documented incident response, data retention policies, or compliance considerations (e.g., GDPR, HIPAA) for handling email data.

scout.summary: The repository is an actively maintained Python CLI tool enabling AI agents to automate interactions with BOSS Zhipin, China's leading job platform. It offers structured JSON output, 34 commands across 8 functional categories (search, apply, chat, AI optimization, etc.), and robust automation features like welfare filtering, pipeline tracking, and incremental monitoring. Recent releases focus on strict type checking improvements.
highlights: Specialized AI agent tool for automating BOSS Zhipin job search, application, and communication workflows.
Rich command set (34 commands) with structured JSON output, supporting search with welfare filters, batch greetings, AI-powered resume/job analysis, and multi-format export.
Active development: recent releases (v1.8.13–v1.8.15) consistently expand strict type checking coverage across the codebase.
Comprehensive documentation and developer setup using modern Python tooling (uv, Patchright).
risks: Potential fragility due to reliance on reverse-engineering a third-party platform (BOSS Zhipin), which may change its interface or block automated access.
Limited community size (66 stars, 8 forks) may impact long-term maintenance and external contributions despite active solo development.

inventor.summary: The repository introduces a novel CLI tool that empowers AI agents to autonomously navigate and interact with BOSS Zhipin, combining job search automation, structured communication, and AI-driven optimization in a single workflow. It innovatively bridges recruitment platform interaction with agent-centric design through strict typing, multi-tier authentication fallbacks, and exportable structured outputs.
highlights: Invents an AI-native interface for BOSS Zhipin by exposing 34 structured commands covering discovery, outreach, pipeline tracking, and AI-enhanced job application tasks.
Integrates welfare-based filtering (e.g., "双休,五险一金") directly into search logic with automatic pagination and matching—uncommon in standard job APIs.
Embeds AI co-pilot capabilities (resume polish, JD analysis, interview prep) as first-class CLI commands, enabling agent-driven career optimization.
Uses modern Python tooling (uv, Patchright) and enforces strict type safety across all commands, indicating deliberate engineering for reliability in autonomous operation.
risks: Core functionality depends on reverse-engineering a proprietary web platform (BOSS Zhipin), making it vulnerable to breaking changes or legal challenges.
Highly specialized use case may limit adoption beyond niche AI agent developers targeting Chinese job markets.

engineer.summary: The repository implements a well-structured, type-safe Python CLI for automating interactions with BOSS Zhipin, featuring comprehensive command coverage, modern tooling (uv, Patchright), and robust automation logic. Recent engineering efforts focus on expanding strict type checking across all commands, indicating a strong emphasis on reliability and maintainability.
highlights: Uses modern Python tooling: project setup relies on uv for dependency management and Patchright (a Playwright fork) for browser automation, ensuring reproducible dev environments.
Enforces strict type safety across the entire CLI command surface, with recent releases incrementally expanding type-checking coverage (v1.8.13–v1.8.15).
Implements complex automation workflows including multi-tier login fallbacks, welfare-based filtering with pagination, and structured JSON output—critical for AI agent integration.
Organized codebase with clear separation of concerns: src/, skills/, extension/, mcp-server/, and thorough documentation (README, CHANGELOG, ROADMAP, CONTRIBUTING).
risks: Heavy reliance on browser automation via Patchright introduces performance overhead and potential instability if BOSS Zhipin modifies its frontend structure or anti-bot measures.
Lack of test coverage visibility: while CI and Codecov badges are present, no evidence shows unit/integration test depth, raising concerns about long-term robustness despite type safety.

skeptic.summary: While the repository presents an ambitious AI agent interface for BOSS Zhipin with extensive CLI capabilities and type safety, its core functionality hinges on fragile reverse-engineering of a third-party platform with no guarantee of stability, legality, or longevity. Claims of robustness are undermined by lack of visible test coverage and overreliance on browser automation.
highlights: The tool’s entire value proposition depends on automating interactions with BOSS Zhipin—a platform not designed for programmatic access—making it inherently vulnerable to breaking changes or blocking.
Despite badges for CI and Codecov [E-RD-001], there is no evidence of meaningful test suites in the provided data, raising doubts about actual reliability despite strict type checking [E-RL-003].
Recent updates focus narrowly on expanding type-checking white lists [E-RL-001, E-RL-002, E-RL-003], which may indicate reactive patching rather than proactive architectural resilience.
The project is maintained almost exclusively by a single contributor (@can4hou6joeng4), with minimal community engagement (66 stars, 8 forks), suggesting limited real-world validation.
risks: Reverse-engineering BOSS Zhipin likely violates its terms of service, exposing users to account bans or legal risk—yet this critical issue is unaddressed in SECURITY.md or documentation.
Browser automation via Patchright [E-RD-006] is brittle; any frontend change on BOSS Zhipin could break core commands like `search`, `greet`, or `apply` without warning.
Structured JSON output assumes stable parsing of dynamic web content—if selectors or APIs shift, outputs may become malformed, misleading AI agents downstream.
No evidence of error handling robustness: 4-tier login fallback [E-RD-003] sounds sophisticated but could mask repeated authentication failures that degrade user trust.

scout.summary: Cerul is an early-stage, multimodal video search platform for AI agents that indexes speech, visuals, and on-screen text. It offers a live API with client libraries (npm/PyPI), a Tavily-inspired playground, and admin analytics. The project is actively developed with frequent releases, uses Cloudflare Workers for deployment, and integrates Gemini models for summarization and relevance filtering.
highlights: Indexes video content beyond transcripts—captures visuals like slides, code, and whiteboards [ID:E-RD-006].
Live API with developer-friendly tooling: playground, syntax-highlighted code snippets, and SDKs on npm/PyPI [ID:E-RD-003][ID:E-RL-001].
Deployed via Cloudflare Workers with Docker images published to GitHub Container Registry [ID:E-RL-002][ID:E-RL-003].
Uses Google’s Gemini models (e.g., 3.1 Flash Lite) for semantic processing and relevance ranking [ID:E-RL-002].
Active development with structured releases featuring analytics, feedback loops, and UX improvements [ID:E-RL-001].
risks: Very early versioning (v0.0.03) suggests instability and potential breaking changes [ID:E-RL-001].
Heavy reliance on proprietary LLMs (Gemini) may introduce cost, latency, or vendor lock-in risks [ID:E-RL-002].
Limited community traction (127 stars, 5 forks) and minimal issue history could indicate low external validation [ID:E-RD-007].
No visible test suite or CI/CD evidence in root files or releases raises quality assurance concerns [ID:E-RF-001].

inventor.summary: Cerul introduces a novel approach to video search for AI agents by unifying multimodal signals—speech, on-screen text, and visual content like slides and code—into a single semantic index. It provides an API-first platform with developer tooling, real-time analytics, and LLM-powered relevance ranking, enabling agents to 'see' and reason over video content beyond transcripts.
highlights: Invents a multimodal video indexing system that captures not just spoken words but also visual evidence such as charts, whiteboards, and code walkthroughs [ID:E-RD-006].
Pioneers an agent-centric video search API with SDKs (npm/PyPI), syntax-highlighted code generation, and a Tavily-inspired playground for rapid prototyping [ID:E-RD-003][ID:E-RL-001].
Integrates generative AI (Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite) directly into the retrieval pipeline for dynamic summarization and relevance filtering [ID:E-RL-002].
Architects a stateless, Cloudflare Workers–based deployment model with containerized worker images, enabling scalable edge inference [ID:E-RL-002][ID:E-RL-003].
Embeds feedback loops via thumbs up/down in the playground and logs result previews, supporting continuous improvement of search quality [ID:E-RL-001].
risks: Core innovation depends on proprietary LLMs (Gemini), risking disruption if model access or pricing changes [ID:E-RL-002].
Early-stage architecture (v0.0.03) lacks evidence of robust testing, version stability, or backward compatibility guarantees [ID:E-RL-001][ID:E-RF-001].
Multimodal indexing claims are compelling but unverified by public benchmarks or technical documentation in the repo [ID:E-RD-006].

engineer.summary: Cerul is an early-stage, multimodal video search API built for AI agents, leveraging Cloudflare Workers and Gemini models to index and retrieve video content by speech, visuals, and on-screen text. It provides developer tooling including SDKs, a playground with code generation, and admin analytics, but lacks visible test infrastructure and relies heavily on proprietary LLMs.
highlights: Deploys as a stateless service on Cloudflare Workers with containerized worker images published to GitHub Container Registry [ID:E-RL-002][ID:E-RL-003].
Uses Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite for in-pipeline summarization and relevance filtering, tightly coupling core functionality to a proprietary LLM [ID:E-RL-002].
Offers a Tavily-inspired API playground with syntax-highlighted code snippets (Python/JS/Shell/Go), result previews, and feedback mechanisms [ID:E-RL-001].
Indexes multimodal video signals—slides, code, whiteboards—beyond transcripts, enabling semantic search over visual content [ID:E-RD-006].
Provides official npm and PyPI packages, indicating commitment to cross-language developer adoption [ID:E-RD-003].
risks: No evidence of testing infrastructure (e.g., unit tests, CI workflows) in root files or releases, raising concerns about reliability and regression control [ID:E-RF-001].
Heavy dependency on Google’s Gemini models introduces vendor lock-in, cost volatility, and potential latency bottlenecks [ID:E-RL-002].
Extremely early versioning (v0.0.03) suggests unstable APIs and frequent breaking changes, unsuitable for production integrations [ID:E-RL-001].
Lack of public technical documentation on multimodal indexing methodology limits verifiability of core claims [ID:E-RD-006].

skeptic.summary: Cerul presents ambitious claims about multimodal video search for AI agents but exhibits significant gaps in verifiability, stability, and engineering rigor that undermine confidence in its readiness or reliability.
highlights: Claims to index visual content like slides and code [ID:E-RD-006], yet provides no public technical details, benchmarks, or validation of this capability.
Promotes a live API and developer tooling [ID:E-RD-003][ID:E-RL-001], but the project is only at v0.0.03—indicating extreme immaturity and likely instability.
Relies on proprietary Gemini models for core functionality [ID:E-RL-002], creating opaque dependencies with no fallback or open alternative.
risks: No visible test suite, CI/CD, or quality gates in root files [ID:E-RF-001], suggesting unverified correctness and high risk of regressions.
Extremely limited community engagement (127 stars, 5 forks, 0 issues) [ID:E-RD-007] raises doubts about real-world validation or adoption.
Core multimodal indexing claims are marketing-oriented and unsupported by evidence in the repository—could be aspirational rather than implemented.
API and deployment model depend on Cloudflare Workers and external LLMs without clear SLAs, cost controls, or offline/self-hosted options [ID:E-RL-002][ID:E-RL-003].
